question.text: As per SEBI regulations, the minimum liquid net worth that every clearing member must maintain with the exchange/clearing corporation is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** All clearing members (both CMs and SCMs) must maintain at least Rs. 50 lakhs as liquid net worth with the exchange/CC. This is separate from the balance sheet net worth requirement (Rs. 3 Cr for CMs, Rs. 1 Cr for SCMs). **The Trap:** Rs. 50 lakhs is LIQUID net worth (deposit), not balance sheet net worth. Rs. 300 lakhs is balance sheet net worth for CMs. **Cross-Exam Context:** Membership eligibility criteria are directly tested regulatory facts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Liquid NW = Rs. 50 lakhs (all CMs). Balance sheet NW = Rs. 3 Cr (CM) / Rs. 1 Cr (SCM).
